generated_from: scripts/scrape-venice-categories.json — Phase 1 baseline; refine after initial-scrape data is in

venice.golden_lion.notes: Golden Lion (Leone d'Oro) — single winner per ceremony in modern era. Tolerance 1 allows for the rare hypothetical tie.

venice.silver_lion_grand_jury.notes: Maps to Wikipedia's 'Grand Jury Prize (Venice Film Festival)' — the second-tier non-director prize. Awarded in most ceremonies, but Venice did not award it in 2007 + 2008 (era_override). Tolerance 1 allows for ties.

era_overrides.reason: Grand Jury Prize was not awarded at Venice in 2007 (64th festival) or 2008 (65th). Confirmed real-data gap from initial Phase 1 scrape.

venice.silver_lion_best_director.notes: Maps to Wikipedia's 'Silver Lion' (target [[Silver Lion]]). Modern era (2013+): person-first 'Silver Lion for Best Director' format. Pre-2013: typically film+director second-tier prize without Best Director qualifier. Tolerance 1 allows for ties.

venice.special_jury_prize.notes: Distinct from Grand Jury Prize — Venice has both. Special Jury Prize was awarded intermittently in the early 2000s (era_overrides for the gap years 2000-2006 + 2009-2011). Awarded as a regular fixture from 2012 onward. Tolerance 1 covers ties.

era_overrides.reason: Special Jury Prize not awarded at Venice 2000-2006. Confirmed real-data gap from initial Phase 1 scrape (Wikipedia ceremony articles for these years do not list a Special Jury Prize bullet).
Special Jury Prize not awarded at Venice 2009-2012. Confirmed real-data gap (extended from initial 2009-2011 range during Phase 4 Batch C truth curation when cer 2012 also surfaced as missing).

venice.volpi_cup_best_actor.notes: Volpi Cup for Best Actor. Cannes-style ties have occurred occasionally; tolerance 1 covers.

venice.volpi_cup_best_actress.notes: Volpi Cup for Best Actress. Cannes-style ties have occurred occasionally; tolerance 1 covers.

venice.best_screenplay.notes: Premio Osella for Best Screenplay. Display name varies historically ('Golden Osella for Outstanding Technical Contribution (screenplay)' in older years). Not awarded as a dedicated screenplay prize in 2001-2004 (the Golden Osella was sometimes given for non-screenplay technical contributions in those years — era_override). Tolerance 1 covers ties / co-author teams.

era_overrides.reason: Best Screenplay (Premio Osella) not awarded as a dedicated screenplay prize at Venice 2001-2004 — pre-Premio-Osella-for-Screenplay era. The Golden Osella in those years was given for other technical contributions (e.g. cer 2004 went to Studio Ghibli for Howl's Moving Castle, an animation/technical award). Confirmed real-data gap from initial Phase 1 scrape.

venice.marcello_mastroianni_award.notes: Spotty history at Venice — has been retired and reinstated; expect 0-row years for some ceremonies. Tolerance 1 allows for ties; era_overrides will likely be added once gap years are identified from initial scrape.

venice.lion_of_the_future.notes: Luigi De Laurentis Award for Debut Feature. Section-heading-driven extraction (Pass 2). Two ceremony-specific gap years documented via era_overrides: 2003 (Wikipedia article gap — section absent from the ceremony article) and 2020 (COVID-restricted ceremony — Lion of the Future not awarded). Single winner expected per ceremony for all other years.

era_overrides.reason: Lion of the Future / Luigi De Laurentis Award absent from cer 2003 Wikipedia article. Either a Wikipedia article completeness gap or a real non-award. Documented in known-issue venice-lion-of-the-future-gaps-2026-05-04.
Cer 2020 (77th Venice) ran on a COVID-restricted format and did not award the Lion of the Future / Luigi De Laurentis Award. Confirmed real-data gap from initial Phase 1 scrape.
